mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls over a quarter reflects robust labor demand and income growth, which supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS 3-month change on 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or hit rate of positive returns following increases is not significantly above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward return is positive in more than 55% of instances after UNRATE rises above 5.5%, or if average return difference pre/post trigger is not negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation as measured by the CPI, accompanied by a federal funds rate that remains below 3.5%, indicates that monetary policy is too loose relative to price pressures, creating inflation risk that depresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over 20 days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy amid rising inflation
failure_condition: If 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL > 195 and FEDFUNDS < 3.5, or if interaction coefficient in linear model is not significantly negative (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5%), further decreases in UNRATE signal an overheating labor market that forces the Fed to tighten policy, leading to negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward return following UNRATE declines in the low unemployment regime is positive in more than 50% of cases, or if average return is not significantly negative relative to high unemployment regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, which foreshadows broader economic weakness and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of instances following a sharp INDPRO decline, or if average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1800 thousand units for two consecutive months, it indicates a severe housing downturn that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances after trigger, or if average return is not significantly negative compared to non-trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a two-month period signal a broad-based demand contraction that weakens corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 0 for 2 months and HOUST MoM growth < 0 for 2 months
failure_condition: If 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of joint decline instances, or if interaction term in regression is not significantly negative (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for FEDFUNDS 3-month change on 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or average return following increases is not significantly negative.
